skill.name: algorithmic-art
skill.description: Create original p5.js generative art with seeded randomness, algorithmic concepts, and interactive parameter exploration.
skill.summary: Create reproducible p5.js generative art with seeded randomness and interactive controls.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: This official Anthropic skill is safe to publish. Static findings are expected for browser-based p5.js templates and CDN links, not malicious behavior. No prompt injection, filesystem access, credential handling, or command execution was found in the reviewed files.

content.value_statement: Generative art can be hard to make reproducible and easy to explore. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create seeded p5.js artworks with interactive controls.
actual_capabilities: Creates an algorithmic philosophy before writing the artwork implementation.
Builds self-contained p5.js HTML artifacts from the included viewer template.
Uses seeded randomness so the same seed reproduces the same composition.
Adds interactive controls for parameters such as particles, noise, color, and speed.
Supports generative techniques such as flow fields, particles, recursion, and harmonics.
Includes seed navigation, regeneration, reset, and PNG download controls.
limitations: The default HTML template loads p5.js and fonts from external CDNs.
It creates interactive sketches, not finished image assets by itself.
Visual quality depends on the user's prompt and the generated implementation.
It avoids copying specific artists or copyrighted works.

use_cases.title: Portfolio Generative Sketch
use_cases.description: Create an original browser-based artwork that a designer can tune and export.

use_cases.title: Interactive Teaching Demo
use_cases.description: Show how noise, particles, and seeded randomness create repeatable visual systems.

use_cases.title: Concept-Driven Art Prototype
use_cases.description: Turn an abstract theme into a philosophy and a working p5.js exploration tool.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Generative Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete interactive sketch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a seeded p5.js generative artwork about [theme]. Include an algorithmic philosophy, interactive controls, and seed navigation.

prompt_templates.title: Flow Field Exploration
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on particles and motion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create algorithmic art using flow fields for [concept]. Make the seed reproducible and expose controls for particle count, noise scale, speed, and color.

prompt_templates.title: Gallery Variation Study
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for exploring many reproducible outputs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a generative art system for [subject] with seed presets, refined parameters, and a clear philosophy that explains the emergent behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Aesthetic System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that requires conceptual translation into code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate [abstract idea] into an original algorithmic art movement, then implement it as a self-contained p5.js artifact using the provided viewer structure.

output_examples.input: Create generative art inspired by tidal memory.
output_examples.output: The result includes a concise philosophy about layered recurrence, plus an interactive p5.js artwork with wave fields, seeded particles, and export controls.

output_examples.input: Make a reproducible flow field study with warm colors.
output: A self-contained browser artifact with seed navigation.
Controls for particle density, field scale, drift, and palette balance.
A downloadable image output from the current seed.

output_examples.input: Explore a crystalline system that feels organic.
output_examples.output: The skill guides a relaxation-based visual system with adjustable growth, spacing, symmetry, and color temperature.

best_practices: Start with a clear theme or concept, then let the skill translate it into algorithmic behavior.
Keep seed values when comparing variations so strong results can be reproduced.
Ask for specific controls when users need to tune density, motion, color, or scale.
anti_patterns: Do not request direct copies of a specific living artist or copyrighted artwork.
Do not remove seed controls when reproducibility matters.
Do not treat the template as optional when creating the HTML artifact.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates an algorithmic philosophy and a self-contained p5.js HTML artwork with interactive controls.

faq.question: Can I reproduce the same artwork later?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill uses seeded randomness so the same seed can recreate the same result.

faq.question: Does it need a build system?
faq.answer: No. The intended output is a single HTML artifact that runs in a browser.

faq.question: Can users change the artwork after it is created?
faq.answer: Yes. The generated artifact includes parameter controls, seed controls, reset, regeneration, and download actions.

faq.question: Does it support animation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create static or animated p5.js sketches depending on the requested concept.

faq.question: Can it imitate a named artist?
faq.answer: No. The skill directs models to create original algorithmic art instead of copying existing artists.
